cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the best title for this passage about the London Olympics. The question gives four options: (A) How to deal with anti-doping, (B) How to use recycled materials, (C) London's green games, and (D) London's public transport. First, I'll read through the passage again to get a sense of the main points. The article starts by saying that while opinions differ on whether the London Olympics was a success, one certainty is that London won a gold medal for hosting the "greenest" Games ever. The key theme here is the green or eco-friendly aspects of the Olympics. The next paragraphs talk about how London used the construction of the Olympic Park to clean up polluted areas, removing 2 million tons of contaminated soil and tearing down 200 old factories. Then it mentions that 99% of the debris were reused, like the steel tubes from old gas pipelines used for the stadium's roof. The article also discusses green building techniques in the venues, such as the Olympic Stadium being the lightest ever built and using less steel and concrete. The handball field had lighting pipes that reduced electricity use. Then there's a section about waste management: using collected rainwater and recycled wastewater to save 30-40% water. Food waste was recycled or turned into energy. Public transport was encouraged to reduce traffic and carbon emissions. Lastly, the article mentions the anti-doping efforts, which were praised by the IOC President because only one athlete tested positive. So, looking at all these points, the article is mainly about the various green initiatives London implemented during the Olympics. The anti-doping part is mentioned but it's a minor point compared to the environmental efforts. The use of recycled materials and public transport are parts of the larger theme of the green Games. Option (C) "London's green games" seems to encapsulate all these aspects. Option (B) is too narrow because it focuses only on recycled materials, which is part of the green initiatives but not the whole story. Option (D) is about public transport, which is
